metadata.name: General Data Protection Regulation
metadata.description: The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is a comprehensive data protection law that applies to organizations processing personal data of individuals in the European Union. It establishes principles for data processing, individual rights, and organizational obligations.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The GDPR is a mandatory regulatory framework enacted as EU law. All organizations processing personal data of EU residents must comply, regardless of where the organization is located. It is enforced by data protection authorities with significant penalty powers.
metadata.framework_purpose: To protect the fundamental rights and freedoms of natural persons, particularly their right to the protection of personal data, while ensuring the free movement of personal data within the European Union.
metadata.framework_scope: All organizations that process personal data of individuals in the European Union, regardless of the organization's location, including controllers and processors of personal data.
framework_objectives: Protect individuals' rights regarding their personal data
Establish uniform data protection rules across the EU
Define lawful bases for processing personal data
Ensure transparency in data processing activities
Enable individuals to exercise control over their personal data
Hold organizations accountable for data protection
metadata.target_audience: Data Protection Officers, Privacy Teams, Legal Counsel, IT Security Teams, and Compliance Officers of any organization processing EU personal data
metadata.regulatory_authority: European Union / European Data Protection Board
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Up to €20 million or 4% of annual global turnover (whichever is higher) for the most serious infringements. Lower tier fines of up to €10 million or 2% of annual global turnover for less severe violations.
metadata.adoption_approach: Mandatory compliance for all organizations processing EU personal data. Organizations must implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance.

sections: Chapter III Rights of the Data Subject
Chapter V Transfers of Personal Data to Third Countries
Chapter VIII Remedies, Liability and Penalties
Chapter IX Provisions Relating to Specific Processing Situations
Chapter X Delegated Acts and Implementing Acts

controls.title: Lawfulness, Fairness and Transparency
controls.description: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be processed lawfully, fairly and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject ('lawfulness, fairness and transparency').

controls.title: Purpose Limitation
controls.description: Personal data shall be collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner that is incompatible with those purposes ('purpose limitation').

controls.title: Data Minimisation
controls.description: Personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed ('data minimisation').

controls.title: Accuracy
controls.description: Personal data shall be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date; every reasonable step must be taken to ensure that personal data that are inaccurate are erased or rectified without delay ('accuracy').

controls.title: Storage Limitation
controls.description: Personal data shall be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which the personal data are processed ('storage limitation').

controls.title: Integrity and Confidentiality
controls.description: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data.
controls.full_text: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage ('integrity and confidentiality').

controls.title: Accountability
controls.description: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, the data protection principles.
controls.full_text: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, paragraph 1 ('accountability').

controls.title: Lawfulness of Processing
controls.description: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one lawful basis applies.
controls.full_text: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one of the following applies: consent, contract necessity, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.

controls.title: Conditions for Consent
controls.description: Where processing is based on consent, the controller must be able to demonstrate consent and ensure it is freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous.
controls.full_text: Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented. Consent must be freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of wishes.

controls.title: Processing of Special Categories of Data
controls.description: Processing of special categories of personal data is prohibited except under specified conditions.
controls.full_text: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data, health data, or sex life/orientation shall be prohibited except under specified lawful exceptions.

controls.title: Transparent Information and Communication
controls.description: Controllers shall take appropriate measures to provide information to data subjects in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form.
controls.full_text: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information and any communication relating to processing in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language.

controls.title: Information When Data Collected from Data Subject
controls.description: When personal data are collected from the data subject, the controller shall provide specified information at the time of collection.
controls.full_text: Where personal data relating to a data subject are collected from the data subject, the controller shall, at the time when personal data are obtained, provide the data subject with identity of controller, purposes, recipients, retention period, and rights information.

controls.title: Information When Data Not Obtained from Data Subject
controls.description: When personal data are not obtained from the data subject, the controller shall provide specified information within a reasonable period.
controls.full_text: Where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject, the controller shall provide the data subject with the required information within a reasonable period after obtaining the data, but at the latest within one month.

controls.title: Right of Access by the Data Subject
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to obtain confirmation of whether personal data are being processed and access to the data.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether or not personal data concerning him or her are being processed, and, where that is the case, access to the personal data and specified information.

controls.title: Right to Rectification
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to obtain rectification of inaccurate personal data.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning him or her.

controls.title: Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten)
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to obtain erasure of personal data under specified circumstances.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller the erasure of personal data concerning him or her without undue delay where one of the specified grounds applies, including where data is no longer necessary or consent is withdrawn.

controls.title: Right to Restriction of Processing
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to obtain restriction of processing under specified circumstances.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller restriction of processing where accuracy is contested, processing is unlawful, or controller no longer needs data but subject requires it for legal claims.

controls.title: Right to Data Portability
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to receive personal data in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to receive the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller, in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format and have the right to transmit those data to another controller.

controls.title: Right to Object
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right to object to processing of personal data for direct marketing or based on legitimate interests.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right to object, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, to processing of personal data based on legitimate interests. Where data are processed for direct marketing, the data subject shall have the right to object at any time.

controls.title: Automated Individual Decision-Making
controls.description: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing that produces legal effects.
controls.full_text: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her.

controls.title: Responsibility of the Controller
controls.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance.
controls.full_text: Taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risks, the controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure and be able to demonstrate that processing is performed in accordance with this Regulation.

controls.title: Data Protection by Design and Default
controls.description: The controller shall implement appropriate measures for implementing data protection principles and ensuring only necessary data is processed.
controls.full_text: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures designed to implement data-protection principles, such as data minimisation, in an effective manner. The controller shall implement measures for ensuring that by default only personal data necessary for each specific purpose are processed.

controls.title: Joint Controllers
controls.description: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall determine their respective responsibilities.
controls.full_text: Where two or more controllers jointly determine the purposes and means of processing, they shall be joint controllers. They shall in a transparent manner determine their respective responsibilities for compliance, including exercise of data subject rights and duties to provide information.

controls.title: Processor Obligations
controls.description: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract binding the processor to the controller.
controls.full_text: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees. Processing shall be governed by a contract that sets out the subject-matter, duration, nature, and purpose of processing.

controls.title: Records of Processing Activities
controls.description: Controllers and processors shall maintain a record of processing activities under their responsibility.
controls.full_text: Each controller and, where applicable, the controller's representative, shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility. The record shall contain specified information including purposes, data categories, recipients, and retention periods.

controls.title: Security of Processing
controls.description: Controllers and processors shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to the risk.
controls.full_text: Taking into account the state of the art, costs of implementation, nature, scope, context and purposes of processing, as well as the risk, the controller and processor shall implement appropriate measures including pseudonymisation, encryption, ability to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability, and ability to restore access.

controls.title: Notification of Personal Data Breach to Supervisory Authority
controls.description: Controllers shall notify the supervisory authority of a personal data breach within 72 hours of becoming aware.
controls.full_text: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority, unless unlikely to result in risk to rights and freedoms.

controls.title: Communication of Personal Data Breach to Data Subject
controls.description: When a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall communicate the breach to the data subject.
controls.full_text: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay.

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
controls.description: Where processing is likely to result in high risk, the controller shall carry out an assessment of the impact on data protection.
controls.full_text: Where a type of processing, in particular using new technologies, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data.

controls.title: Prior Consultation
controls.description: The controller shall consult the supervisory authority prior to processing where a DPIA indicates high residual risk.
controls.full_text: The controller shall consult the supervisory authority prior to processing where a data protection impact assessment indicates that the processing would result in a high risk in the absence of measures taken by the controller to mitigate the risk.

controls.title: Designation of Data Protection Officer
controls.description: Controllers and processors shall designate a DPO in specified circumstances.
controls.full_text: The controller and the processor shall designate a data protection officer in any case where: processing is carried out by a public authority; core activities require regular and systematic monitoring on a large scale; or core activities consist of large-scale processing of special categories of data.

controls.title: Position of the Data Protection Officer
controls.description: The controller and processor shall ensure the DPO is properly involved and supported.
controls.full_text: The controller and processor shall ensure that the data protection officer is involved, properly and in a timely manner, in all issues which relate to the protection of personal data. The DPO shall directly report to the highest management level.

controls.title: Tasks of the Data Protection Officer
controls.description: The DPO shall have defined tasks including informing, advising, monitoring compliance, and cooperating with supervisory authority.
controls.full_text: The data protection officer shall have at least the following tasks: inform and advise on obligations; monitor compliance including awareness-raising and training; provide advice on DPIAs; cooperate with supervisory authority; act as contact point for supervisory authority.

controls.title: General Principle for Transfers
controls.description: Transfers of personal data to third countries shall only take place subject to conditions in Chapter V.
controls.full_text: Any transfer of personal data which are undergoing processing or are intended for processing after transfer to a third country or to an international organisation shall take place only if the conditions laid down in this Chapter are complied with by the controller and processor.

controls.title: Transfers on Basis of Adequacy Decision
controls.description: A transfer may take place where the Commission has decided that the third country ensures an adequate level of protection.
controls.full_text: A transfer of personal data to a third country or an international organisation may take place where the Commission has decided that the third country, a territory or one or more specified sectors within that third country, or the international organisation in question ensures an adequate level of protection.

controls.title: Transfers Subject to Appropriate Safeguards
controls.description: In absence of adequacy decision, transfers may take place where appropriate safeguards are provided.
controls.full_text: In the absence of a decision pursuant to Article 45, a controller or processor may transfer personal data to a third country or an international organisation only if the controller or processor has provided appropriate safeguards, such as standard contractual clauses or binding corporate rules.

controls.title: Binding Corporate Rules
controls.description: Binding corporate rules shall be approved where they meet specified requirements.
controls.full_text: The competent supervisory authority shall approve binding corporate rules in accordance with the consistency mechanism if they are legally binding, confer enforceable rights on data subjects, and meet the requirements of this Article.

controls.title: Derogations for Specific Situations
controls.description: In absence of adequacy decision or appropriate safeguards, transfers may take place only under specific derogations.
controls.full_text: In the absence of an adequacy decision or appropriate safeguards, a transfer may take place only if: data subject explicitly consented; transfer is necessary for contract performance; transfer is necessary for important public interest reasons; or transfer is necessary for vital interests.

controls.title: Right to Lodge Complaint with Supervisory Authority
controls.description: Every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
controls.full_text: Without prejudice to any other administrative or judicial remedy, every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement.

controls.title: Right to Compensation and Liability
controls.description: Any person who has suffered damage as a result of an infringement shall have the right to receive compensation.
controls.full_text: Any person who has suffered material or non-material damage as a result of an infringement of this Regulation shall have the right to receive compensation from the controller or processor for the damage suffered.

controls.title: General Conditions for Administrative Fines
controls.description: Each supervisory authority shall ensure that administrative fines are effective, proportionate and dissuasive.
controls.full_text: Each supervisory authority shall ensure that the imposition of administrative fines in each individual case shall be effective, proportionate and dissuasive. Fines up to €20,000,000 or 4% of total worldwide annual turnover may be imposed for the most serious infringements.
